title: TNGIC and Statewide Geospatial Professional Service

yaml.title: TNGIC and Statewide Geospatial Professional Service
yaml.description: Professional service supporting Tennessee’s geospatial community through TNGIC leadership, conference planning, education and outreach, student engagement, and statewide GIS partnerships.

markdown.markdown:
The **TNGIC and Statewide Geospatial Professional Service** page highlights my service to Tennessee’s geospatial professional community through the **Tennessee Geographic Information Council**, education and outreach work, conference planning, student engagement, and statewide partnerships.

This work reflects my commitment to connecting students, educators, alumni, professionals, employers, and public agencies across Tennessee’s GIS community.


The Tennessee Geographic Information Council plays an important role in connecting GIS professionals, educators, students, vendors, public agencies, private companies, nonprofit organizations, and local governments across the state.

My service with TNGIC has focused on building those connections, supporting professional development, increasing student participation, strengthening education and outreach, and helping the annual conference serve as a meaningful gathering point for Tennessee’s geospatial community.

## TNGIC Leadership

My TNGIC service has included serving on the **TNGIC Board of Directors** and contributing to conference planning, education and outreach, student engagement, and statewide professional programming.

A major part of this work involves helping create opportunities for students and early-career professionals to participate in the state’s geospatial community. This includes promoting conference participation, connecting students with professionals, supporting competitions and fellowships, and helping students see professional GIS pathways beyond the classroom.

## Annual Conference Planning

I served as **Conference Chair** for the **2024** and **2025 TNGIC Annual Conferences**.

Both conferences reached record attendance, and a significant portion of attendees were either students or alumni from the Department of Geography and Sustainability at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville.

This work included:

- conference planning and coordination,
- working with the TNGIC Board of Directors,
- supporting program development,
- recruiting participation,
- helping coordinate student involvement,
- supporting sponsor and partner relationships,
- helping the conference serve both professionals and students.

## Education and Outreach

Education and outreach have been central to my professional service. Through TNGIC and related partnerships, I have worked to support educators, students, and early-career GIS professionals.

This work has included:

- promoting student participation in TNGIC,
- supporting TNView opportunities through TNGIC,
- helping recruit students for fellowships and competitions,
- connecting students with professional mentors and employers,
- supporting the Tennessee ArcGIS Online Middle and High School Mapping Contest,
- maintaining relationships among K–12 educators, university programs, professional GIS organizations, and industry partners.

## Tennessee ArcGIS Online Mapping Contest

The **Tennessee ArcGIS Online Middle and High School Mapping Contest** is one of the long-running education and outreach efforts connected to this service work. I have hosted the contest annually since 2017 in partnership with the Tennessee Geographic Alliance, TNGIC, and Esri.

When Esri stopped funding the contest in 2024, I helped find replacement support through TNGIC so the contest could continue. This work helps K–12 students and teachers engage with GIS, spatial thinking, and geospatial storytelling.

## Student and Alumni Engagement

A major goal of my TNGIC service is helping students move from the classroom into the professional geospatial community.

This includes:

- recruiting students to attend and present at TNGIC,
- connecting students with alumni and employers,
- helping students understand professional conference expectations,
- supporting student research and applied GIS presentations,
- promoting opportunities through TNView and other partners,
- maintaining relationships with alumni who remain active in Tennessee’s GIS community.

## Service Significance

This service work reflects my commitment to building a strong, connected geospatial community in Tennessee. TNGIC provides an important bridge between students, educators, government agencies, private companies, nonprofits, and university programs.

My work with TNGIC combines professional leadership, conference planning, education and outreach, student mentoring, and partnership development. It supports not only the organization, but also the broader pipeline of geospatial education and professional development across the state.
